digest.component_or_area: reconciler baseline convergence rollout / outbound field-differ arbitration
propositions: Remove the two phased rollout flags baseline_dual_write and baseline_consumer_swap from schema, parser, and rebar.toml, making them unknown-and-ignored keys.
Hardcode baseline dual-write and consume as unconditional, collapsing the boolean threading through the outbound field-differ and removing dead back-out branches.
Preserve the per-binding baseline advancement (set_baseline) by extracting it into a standalone helper called unconditionally, while deleting only the dead equivalence-check shadow machinery.
Make the cold-start local-wins window observable by emitting a RECON: baseline_cold_start stderr line when get_baseline() is None for a confirmed binding, and document the warm-up window.
Prove reconcile dry-run output is byte-identical before and after the change via a new fixture-pinned before/after diff test.
Change is backward-compatible because baselines are persisted additively to bindings.json with no store-version bump and the reconciler runs as a single advisory-locked process.
